title: Use invalid() opcode for unrecoverable precompile failures to consume all gas
description: The invalid() opcode (0xfe) consumes all remaining gas and reverts. Used after precompile failures because: (1) precompile failure is an exceptional condition that should never happen in normal operation, (2) consuming all gas prevents the caller from catching the error in a try/catch and proceeding with incorrect data, (3) it's cheaper than writing and reverting with a custom error for a path that should be unreachable.
when_to_apply: Precompile call failures (SHA256, ecrecover, identity) where the failure is considered a critical, unrecoverable error
when_not_to_apply: User-facing errors where the revert reason matters, or when partial gas refund is desired
